projects.name: Project Alpha Demo
projects.description: Core platform modernization program used as the primary demo workspace.

likelihood.label: Rare
likelihood.description: Unlikely to occur except in exceptional circumstances.

likelihood.label: Unlikely
likelihood.description: Could occur, but not expected during normal execution.

likelihood.label: Possible
likelihood.description: Reasonably plausible and should be considered in planning.

likelihood.label: Likely
likelihood.description: Expected to occur in many realistic scenarios.

likelihood.label: Almost Certain
likelihood.description: Expected to occur frequently or is already emerging.

impact.label: Minimal
impact.description: Minor effect with little operational disruption.

impact.label: Minor
impact.description: Limited impact requiring small local adjustments.

impact.label: Moderate
impact.description: Noticeable impact to delivery, cost, or performance.

impact.label: Major
impact.description: Serious disruption requiring management attention and recovery effort.

impact.label: Severe
impact.description: Critical impact to mission, compliance, or core service continuity.

risks.title: Zero-Trust Rollout Causes API Breakage
risks.statement: IF downstream services are not updated for per-session verification before cutover, THEN inter-service authentication failures could spike during rollout, RESULTING IN customer-facing outages and emergency rollback pressure.
risks.trigger: downstream services are not updated for per-session verification before cutover
risks.consequence: inter-service authentication failures could spike during rollout
risks.resultingIn: customer-facing outages and emergency rollback pressure
risks.mitigation: Complete service compatibility tests, stage token validation fallbacks, and require go-live evidence from each dependent team.
risks.contingency: Rollback traffic to the legacy token path and freeze rollout if authentication errors exceed the threshold for ten minutes.

risks.title: Data Residency Review Changes Deployment Dates
risks.statement: IF counsel requests additional control evidence for the regional hosting design, THEN environment approvals may slip, RESULTING IN delayed release readiness and increased coordination overhead.
risks.trigger: counsel requests additional control evidence for the regional hosting design
risks.resultingIn: delayed release readiness and increased coordination overhead
risks.mitigation: Pre-stage residency evidence, map datasets by region, and review control narratives with counsel ahead of sign-off.
risks.contingency: Hold the regulated release train and split unaffected capabilities into a separate deployment.

risks.title: Release Window Overlap With Customer Launch
risks.statement: IF the platform cutover overlaps the customer launch campaign, THEN operational teams may not absorb issues fast enough, RESULTING IN noisy escalation and missed onboarding targets.
risks.trigger: the platform cutover overlaps the customer launch campaign
risks.consequence: operational teams may not absorb issues fast enough
risks.resultingIn: noisy escalation and missed onboarding targets
risks.mitigation: Separate the launch wave from the platform cutover and staff a focused hypercare period.
risks.contingency: Push the external launch by one week if release readiness remains amber.

decisions.title: Adopt Zero-Trust Protocol V2
decisions.summary: Require per-session verification across internal services and retire long-lived tokens.
decisions.context: Identity architecture review found inconsistent session handling across multiple internal APIs. The program needs one standard control model before the phased rollout reaches customer-facing services.
decisionDrivers: Reduce lateral movement risk from compromised credentials
Create one identity standard for platform and product teams
Support the shared rollout plan used by multiple projects
consideredOptions: Adopt per-session verification across all internal services
Add monitoring only and keep long-lived tokens
Pilot zero-trust on privileged services only
decisions.outcome: The council approved per-session verification across all internal services and retired long-lived tokens. Partial rollout was rejected because it would leave too many dependencies on the older model during cutover.
goodConsequences: Creates a consistent control pattern across teams
Can be reused by subscriber projects with minimal rework
decisions.moreInfo: This is the source shared decision for SH-DEC-0001.

decisions.title: Stage Regional Data Residency Migration
decisions.summary: Move regulated datasets into region-specific hosting boundaries ahead of policy deadlines.
decisions.context: Policy guidance is still evolving, but legal review recommended region-specific storage as the least disruptive compliant target architecture.
decisionDrivers: Reduce compliance ambiguity
Avoid mid-cycle remediation work
Preserve a clear audit trail
consideredOptions: Migrate regulated datasets by region
Handle exceptions manually
Delay migration until policy is final
decisions.outcome: The team chose to migrate regulated datasets by region before the next regulated release window.
badConsequences: Consumes delivery capacity during the migration window
decisions.moreInfo: Migration plan validated during release planning.

projects.name: Project Beta Demo
projects.description: Client portal and onboarding program that consumes shared platform controls from Alpha.

likelihood.description: Unlikely to occur except in exceptional circumstances.

likelihood.label: Unlikely
likelihood.description: Could occur, but not expected during normal execution.

risks.sharedOverrideRationale: Portal-specific mitigation adds customer communications and feature flag staging while keeping the upstream risk statement intact.
risks.title: Zero-Trust Rollout Causes Portal Sign-In Failures
risks.statement: IF downstream services are not updated for per-session verification before cutover, THEN portal sign-in and session refresh failures could spike during rollout, RESULTING IN onboarding disruption and customer support load.
risks.trigger: downstream services are not updated for per-session verification before cutover
risks.consequence: portal sign-in and session refresh failures could spike during rollout
risks.resultingIn: onboarding disruption and customer support load
risks.mitigation: Run portal canary releases behind feature flags, pre-stage support messaging, and require synthetic sign-in checks before each rollout wave.
risks.contingency: Disable the new session path for the portal and route traffic through the legacy login stack if error rates rise.

history.label: Linked local risk created from shared risk SH-RSK-0001

risks.title: Localized Content Misses Launch Window
risks.statement: IF translation approvals arrive after the portal release candidate is cut, THEN customer journeys may launch with incomplete localized content, RESULTING IN rework and delayed regional adoption.
risks.trigger: translation approvals arrive after the portal release candidate is cut
risks.consequence: customer journeys may launch with incomplete localized content
risks.mitigation: Prioritize legal and marketing review for the top languages and hold low-volume locales for the follow-on release if needed.
risks.contingency: Launch with English fallback only in low-volume regions after stakeholder approval.

risks.title: Call Center Capacity Lags Portal Launch
risks.statement: IF support training trails the portal launch, THEN the service desk may not absorb early customer confusion, RESULTING IN longer queues and lower launch confidence.
risks.trigger: support training trails the portal launch
risks.consequence: the service desk may not absorb early customer confusion
risks.resultingIn: longer queues and lower launch confidence
risks.mitigation: Schedule live support simulations and align launch staffing with the cutover calendar.
risks.contingency: Throttle the rollout cohort size if service levels fall below target.

decisions.title: Adopt Zero-Trust Protocol V2 For Portal Services
decisions.summary: Use the Alpha-shared identity decision for portal authentication and rollout controls.
decisions.context: The client portal depends on Alpha platform identity services, so the subscriber project adopted the same shared decision and layered project-specific rollout controls on top.
decisionDrivers: Stay aligned with the upstream platform identity control
Reduce local design churn
Create one communications and support plan for the rollout
consideredOptions: Adopt the shared decision from Alpha
Delay portal participation until the second rollout wave
Keep the legacy session model locally
decisions.outcome: Beta adopted the shared Alpha decision and added local portal rollout safeguards. Deferring the portal was rejected because it would increase divergence from the platform baseline.
goodConsequences: Keeps Beta aligned with the source platform decision
badConsequences: Portal timing is now coupled to upstream cutover readiness
decisions.moreInfo: This is a local project decision linked to shared decision SH-DEC-0001.

decisions.title: Split Launch Into Two Cohorts
decisions.summary: Launch the portal to a smaller first cohort before the broad regional rollout.
decisions.context: The program wanted a safer launch ramp while translation and support readiness stabilized.
decisionDrivers: Reduce initial support load
Create room for translation corrections
Preserve launch momentum
consideredOptions: Use two launch cohorts
Launch to all regions at once
Delay the entire launch
decisions.outcome: The PMO approved a two-cohort launch plan to reduce risk while preserving customer momentum.
goodConsequences: Provides a contained first wave for learning
badConsequences: Creates a longer launch management period

projects.name: Project Gamma Demo
projects.description: Analytics and data operations project that also publishes shared controls and decisions.

risks.title: Reference Data Drift Breaks Downstream Analytics
risks.statement: IF master reference data is refreshed without downstream mapping validation, THEN analytics and reporting jobs may misclassify critical records, RESULTING IN incorrect decisions and recovery effort across dependent teams.
risks.trigger: master reference data is refreshed without downstream mapping validation
risks.consequence: analytics and reporting jobs may misclassify critical records
risks.resultingIn: incorrect decisions and recovery effort across dependent teams
risks.mitigation: Add schema and mapping validation gates before each reference data publication and alert subscribers on breaking changes.
risks.contingency: Revert the reference data package and suspend downstream refresh jobs until mapping validation passes.

risks.title: ETL Capacity Falls Behind Forecast
risks.statement: IF reporting demand grows faster than planned ETL capacity, THEN overnight processing may overrun the reporting window, RESULTING IN stale dashboards and delayed business decisions.
risks.trigger: reporting demand grows faster than planned ETL capacity
risks.consequence: overnight processing may overrun the reporting window
risks.resultingIn: stale dashboards and delayed business decisions
risks.mitigation: Tune batch windows and reallocate capacity to the highest-value pipelines.
risks.contingency: Delay lower-priority dashboard refreshes until the next cycle.

decisions.title: Publish Reference Data Contract Checks
decisions.summary: Require validation of schema and mapping contracts before each shared reference data release.
decisions.context: Multiple projects rely on shared reference data packages from Gamma. The board wanted one reusable control to reduce downstream reconciliation effort.
decisionDrivers: Protect downstream consumers from breaking mapping changes
Create one publish gate for reference data releases
Support repeatable governance across subscriber projects
consideredOptions: Require contract checks before each release
Rely on subscriber-side validation only
Freeze reference data changes until the next quarter
decisions.outcome: Gamma approved a release gate that validates schema and mapping contracts before each shared reference data publication.
badConsequences: Adds a release gate to reference data changes
decisions.moreInfo: This is the source shared decision for SH-DEC-0002.

projects.name: Project Delta Demo
projects.description: Field operations rollout used to test subscriber projects, linked local risks, and selective HTML export.

risks.sharedOverrideRationale: Field operations added local safeguards for offline sync and dispatch timing while keeping the upstream data quality trigger.
risks.title: Reference Data Drift Breaks Dispatch Rules
risks.statement: IF master reference data is refreshed without downstream mapping validation, THEN field dispatch rules may classify jobs incorrectly, RESULTING IN missed appointments and manual replanning.
risks.trigger: master reference data is refreshed without downstream mapping validation
risks.consequence: field dispatch rules may classify jobs incorrectly
risks.mitigation: Validate field mapping packages in the dispatch sandbox and require subscriber sign-off before each reference data release reaches production routes.
risks.contingency: Freeze dispatch updates and rerun the last known-good mapping package while operations manually triage affected routes.

history.label: Linked local risk created from shared risk SH-RSK-0002

risks.title: Field Device Stock Arrives Late
risks.statement: IF replacement field devices arrive after technician onboarding begins, THEN mobile rollout readiness will slip, RESULTING IN manual workarounds and reduced adoption.
risks.trigger: replacement field devices arrive after technician onboarding begins
risks.mitigation: Stage spare devices and confirm supplier delivery status twice weekly.
risks.contingency: Borrow stock from the training pool for wave one technicians.

decisions.title: Adopt Shared Reference Data Contract Checks
decisions.summary: Use Gamma's shared data publication checks for field dispatch integrations.
decisions.context: Delta relies on Gamma-published reference data to classify jobs and dispatch technicians. The project adopted the shared decision and added a field-operations validation step.
decisionDrivers: Reduce dispatch disruption from data drift
Stay aligned with Gamma's shared publication control
Create one subscriber acceptance workflow
consideredOptions: Adopt Gamma's shared decision
Build a Delta-only validation rule set
Accept post-release reconciliation
decisions.outcome: Delta adopted the shared Gamma publication decision and added a local dispatch sandbox check before release.
goodConsequences: Aligns Delta with the upstream shared control
Adds a focused local field validation step
badConsequences: Introduces another operational sign-off during release week
decisions.moreInfo: This is a subscriber project decision linked to SH-DEC-0002.

risks.responseSummary: Add schema and mapping validation gates before each reference data publication and alert subscribers on breaking changes.

riskSubscriptions.reviewComment: Portal rollout controls aligned to the shared change with no upstream edits needed.

decisions.title: Adopt Zero-Trust Protocol V2
decisions.context: Identity architecture review found inconsistent session handling across multiple internal APIs. The program needed one shared decision that subscriber projects could reuse during rollout.
decisionDrivers: Reduce credential replay risk
Create one identity control standard
Support multiple rollout projects with the same governance decision
consideredOptions: Adopt per-session verification across all internal services
Add monitoring only and keep long-lived tokens
Pilot zero-trust on privileged services only
decisions.outcome: Alpha approved per-session verification across all internal services and published the decision for subscriber projects to reuse.
goodConsequences: Creates a reusable decision for dependent teams

decisions.title: Publish Reference Data Contract Checks
decisions.context: Gamma needed one shared data publication decision for projects that depend on shared reference data packages.
decisionDrivers: Protect downstream consumers from breaking mapping changes
Create a reusable publication control
Reduce reconciliation effort after releases
consideredOptions: Require contract checks before each release
Rely on subscriber-side validation only
Freeze reference data changes
decisions.outcome: Gamma approved contract checks before each shared reference data release and published the decision for subscriber projects to adopt.
goodConsequences: Creates one reusable governance control for reference data
badConsequences: Adds a release gate to data changes
